rationale: Neither candidate hits the one substantive ground-truth concern (whether hasattr-only checks for reduce_scatter are sufficient verification), so recall is low for both. X engages more with test-coverage gaps in the same area (missing cfg=1 case, untested None/world_size<=1 branches, fragile order assertion) and delivers five concrete, grounded, actionable file/line suggestions, whereas Y offers deeper API verification (confirming DPMetadata's fields and init_model_parallel_group's signature) but converges to a single actionable comment plus an APPROVE verdict, making it less actionable despite comparable groundedness.
